qa.user_verbatim_complaint: commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode never writes the <!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} --> marker required by commands/commit.md Step 7 Stage 3. Fix the producer.
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: The instruction was added to commands/spec-update.md lines 54-62 within ## Continuation-spec mode section, directing the agent to write the marker before the first new cycle block. The instruction addresses the root cause: the missing producer-side instruction that commit.md Stage 3 requires.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: grep -n 'spec-continuation-of' commands/spec-update.md returns line 55, confirmed inside ## Continuation-spec mode (line 31) before ## Temp-note mode (line 93)

qa.overall_assessment: The prose instruction change is functionally correct: the marker text, idempotency, mode restriction, and no-literal-placeholder requirements are all present in commands/spec-update.md. AC4 (static grep) passes. However, all 5 generated test files for this task contain TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels — dev was required to fill in test bodies but did not do so. Per QA Phase 5 manifest verification, every active TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel is a critical dev incompleteness finding. Additionally, AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5 cover runtime behavior that requires a live spec-update run to fully verify; QA verified these only at the instruction-contract level (static prose inspection). Verdict is FAIL due to the TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels in active test files.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: NO producer ever writes this marker
success_criteria_results.criterion: After spec-update continuation run for task-id T, spec file contains <!-- spec-continuation-of: T --> with resolved value
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Static instruction-contract inspection of commands/spec-update.md lines 54-62
success_criteria_results.details: Instruction present at lines 54-62. States: check for existing marker, write if absent, write 'as the very first line of the new cycle block, before any section headings', substitute the actual task-id value, never write literal placeholder. All four requirements from the AC are encoded. AC1 test (test_AC1_21508659cd0bd681.py) carries TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel — runtime verification not performed by dev.
success_criteria_results.evidence: sed -n '54,62p' commands/spec-update.md confirms all instruction elements present

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Stage 3 of every future /commit will find zero candidates
success_criteria_results.criterion: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->' <spec_path> exits 0 after spec-update continuation run
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Static: confirmed instruction text will produce the marker grep-lF can match. Runtime: not exercised.
success_criteria_results.details: The instruction produces the correct marker format at runtime. commit.md Stage 3 (line 164) greps for '<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->' where ${TASK_ID} is a shell variable expanded to the resolved value. The instruction in spec-update.md directs writing the same resolved value. Runtime output will be identical. AC2 test (test_AC2_1cac8538f4fa4289.py) carries TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel.
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep confirmed commit.md:164 and spec-update.md:55 instruction text; both describe the same runtime output

qa._success_criteria_rule: 2 requirements from BA requirements_decomposition verified at instruction-contract level. Runtime verification requires live spec-update run.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause is confirmed addressed at the instruction-contract level: the missing producer instruction is now present at commands/spec-update.md lines 54-62. The instruction satisfies all four requirements (idempotency, mode restriction, no literal placeholder, marker before first section). The chain from spec-update.md instruction to commit.md Stage 3 discovery is sound per BA QA report verification of commit.md:188 dispatch path.

regression_test_results.details: commands/spec-update.md appears in git diff f79fba1c1684d246928a3cfb39bf1ed7a9a5c8a5 --name-only output. File is confirmed changed from baseline. diff shows 12 lines added, 2 removed at lines 49-63 — the new marker instruction paragraph.

dev.files_modified contains only commands/spec-update.md. The change is purely additive prose (new paragraph inserted). No removal of existing instructions. All callers (dev.md, close.md, dev-command.md, commit.md Stage 7 inline Agent) continue to function; the new step is additional.

e2e_enforcement.blocking_reason: Agent-prompt instruction change only. No UI, no web service, no user-facing code path affected. Skip condition applies per QA Step 10: 'pure offline scripts that users never invoke, documentation-only changes'. This change instructs an AI agent; it is not a web page, API, or user-interactive component.

blast_radius_phase2._note: blast-radius-tool.py not invoked — dev.blast_radius_declarations is empty and the single modified file (commands/spec-update.md, pure Markdown instruction) has no import chain, no runtime code paths, and no coverage gap risk. ac4_static_verification.description: AC4 is the only statically verifiable AC. Verified directly.
ac4_static_verification.evidence: Single occurrence at line 55, between line 31 (## Continuation-spec mode) and line 93 (## Temp-note mode). awk filter of Temp-note mode section returns no spec-continuation-of matches.

idempotency_check_present.evidence: Line 59: 'If it is already present, do not write a second copy.'

scope_restriction_to_continuation_mode.evidence: Lines 60-61: 'This marker is written exactly once per (task-id, spec file) pair and must appear only in this continuation-spec mode, never in temp-note mode.'

no_literal_placeholder_prohibition.evidence: Lines 56-57: '(substituting the actual task-id value — never a literal `${TASK_ID}` or `<task-id>` placeholder)'

placement_before_section_content.evidence: Line 57-58: 'write it as the very first line of the new cycle block, before any section headings'. Instruction block (lines 54-62) precedes 'Populate the 8-section template' (line 63).

marker_format_correct.evidence: Line 55 shows: '<!-- spec-continuation-of: <resolved-task-id> -->' with explicit note that <resolved-task-id> is to be substituted with the actual value. Runtime output matches commit.md:164 grep target when resolved.

notation_drift_analysis.codex_assessment: NOT a blocking issue. Both forms are documentation-level variable notation; both resolve to the same wire string at runtime. spec-update.md:56-57 explicitly prohibits writing placeholders.
notation_drift_analysis.qa_assessment: Notation drift is a documentation quality concern (spec_text_vs_execution_drift dimension) but does NOT cause runtime mismatch. An agent reading spec-update.md would produce the resolved marker identical to what commit.md Stage 3 greps for. Not raising as a blocking finding.
notation_drift_analysis.codex_proposed_hardening: Rewrite to include concrete example and forbid all placeholder forms including <resolved-task-id> itself. OBSERVATION_ONLY — no PROPOSED_FIX that is blocking.

all_findings.issue: Active generated test contains TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel — dev did not implement the test body. Per QA Phase 5 manifest verification rule, every active TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel is a critical dev incompleteness finding.
all_findings.recommendation: Dev must implement the test body: create a disposable spec file, write the marker instruction output manually, run grep -F to confirm presence, assert exit code 0. Alternatively, if this AC requires a live agent run, the test should be archived as manual/non-gating and a comment added explaining why.

all_findings.issue: Active generated test contains TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel — AC2 test not implemented by dev.
all_findings.recommendation: Create a temp spec file with the marker, run grep -lF, assert exit 0. This can be done without a live agent run by synthesizing the minimal fixture.

all_findings.issue: Active generated test contains TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel — AC3 test not implemented by dev.
all_findings.recommendation: Create a temp spec file without literal placeholder, run two negative greps (exit 1 each). Fully static and implementable without live run.

all_findings.issue: Active generated test contains TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel — AC4 test not implemented by dev. This AC is purely statically verifiable (grep commands/spec-update.md for section boundaries) and dev explicitly acknowledged it as static in qa_notes. There is no excuse for leaving this test incomplete.
all_findings.recommendation: Implement: grep -n 'spec-continuation-of' commands/spec-update.md, assert all line numbers fall between ## Continuation-spec mode (line 31) and ## Temp-note mode (line 93). This is a 5-line test.

all_findings.issue: Active generated test contains TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel — AC5 idempotency test not implemented by dev.
all_findings.recommendation: Create a temp spec file with the marker already present, simulate second run by attempting to add again (check that grep -c returns 1). Implementable as a fixture-based unit test.

all_findings.issue: AC5 verification recipe uses 'grep -c' which counts matching lines not exact occurrences. Two markers on one line (pathological edge case) would count as 1, passing falsely. Additionally, the grep pattern 'spec-continuation-of: T' is shorter than the full marker string.
all_findings.recommendation: Replace with: grep -oF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->' <spec_path> | wc -l must equal 1. This is an observation per codex; the literal AC text should be updated by BA.

all_findings.issue: The prose example of the marker uses '<resolved-task-id>' as the variable placeholder. While lines 56-57 prohibit ${TASK_ID} and <task-id> as literals, they do not explicitly prohibit <resolved-task-id> as a literal. An overly literal agent might write the example verbatim.
all_findings.recommendation: Codex proposed_fix: rewrite to include a concrete example (e.g., '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260524-125300-C -->') and extend the prohibition list to include '<resolved-task-id>' explicitly. This is a hardening improvement, not a correctness bug for a competent agent.

failures.issue: All 5 active generated tests contain TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels. Dev was required by QA Step 11 Phase 5 to implement test bodies before submitting for QA. pytest runs with exit code 1: 5 failures, 0 passes.
failures.recommendation: Dev must implement all 5 test bodies. AC4 is fully static (no live run needed). AC3 can be implemented with a temp fixture file. AC1/AC2/AC5 require either a minimal fixture-based approach (create a temp file, write marker, grep) or be explicitly archived as manual/non-gating with orchestrator approval.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed: (1) notation drift between <resolved-task-id> and ${TASK_ID} is NOT a blocking issue — both resolve to the same wire string at runtime; (2) minor wording hardening recommended: prohibit <resolved-task-id> itself as a literal in the prohibition list; (3) AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5 unverifiable by static inspection alone — PASS defensible only if instruction-contract verification is acceptable for prose-only changes; (4) active TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels in all 5 generated tests are a process blocker if generated tests are gating (which they are per QA Phase 5 contract); (5) AC5 grep-c is imprecise — should use grep -oF | wc -l.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1 and 3 incorporated: draft changed from FAIL (notation drift) to FAIL (TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels) — the actual blocker is the active tests, not the notation drift. Finding 2 incorporated as minor finding (not blocking). Finding 4 confirmed as the primary blocker. Finding 5 incorporated as minor finding. Final verdict: FAIL with 5 critical issues (all TEST_INCOMPLETE), 2 minor issues.

failed_criteria: QA Phase 5 manifest verification: all 5 active generated tests contain TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels (test_AC1 through test_AC5). Pytest exit code 1.
AC4 test (test_AC4_a80369928df6194f.py) is statically verifiable but dev left it with TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel despite acknowledging it as static in qa_notes.
critical_issues: 5 active TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels in tests/generated/20260524-125300-C/. All must be implemented or explicitly archived as manual/non-gating per orchestrator decision.
AC4 test body is trivially implementable (grep commands/spec-update.md + assert line numbers in range). Dev acknowledged it as static but did not implement.
AC3 test body is implementable with a temp fixture file. No live run required.
AC1/AC2/AC5 test bodies require either minimal fixture-based approach (create temp file, write marker, grep) or explicit archival as manual.
refined_context.recommended_approach: Dev should implement at minimum: (1) test_AC4: grep -n 'spec-continuation-of' commands/spec-update.md, assert line numbers 31-93; (2) test_AC3: create temp file without placeholder forms, run two negative greps; (3) test_AC1: create temp spec file, simulate what spec-update would write (the marker line), grep to confirm. For AC2 and AC5, either implement similarly or request orchestrator approval to archive as manual tests. The core instruction change in commands/spec-update.md is correct — only the test scaffolding is missing.
refined_context.additional_context: The prose instruction change itself is functionally sound. If the orchestrator determines that TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels are not gating for this MICRO task (because AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5 inherently require an agent run), it may choose to close with the caveat that generated tests are manual. QA is flagging per contract; the orchestrator holds the authority to override for MICRO documentation-only changes.
